2567	\section{Pullback-monoidality: invertibility under a general scheme morphism}
2568	\label{sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality}
2569	
2570	The relative Picard consumer (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) re-bases its
2571	line-bundle carrier onto the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
2572	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}, and its
2573	functorial structure maps are \emph{pullback} maps of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the
2574	projection pullback \(\pi_T^*\) along \(C \times_S T \to T\) and the base-change
2575	pullback along \(C \times_S T' \to C \times_S T\). For these maps to descend to the
2576	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) carrier one needs that \emph{pullback preserves
2577	\(\otimes\)-invertibility} for \emph{general} morphisms of schemes --- the
2578	projection and the base-change morphism are neither open immersions nor flat. This
2579	is the target lemma \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} of the section.
2580	
2581	This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
2582	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
2583	restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
2584	\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
2585	strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
2586	map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)). A general scheme
2587	morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable.
2588	
2589	\paragraph{The reduction to two general facts.} The target
2590	\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is a three-line consequence of two facts about
2591	pullback, exactly as in the Stacks proof of \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}. The
2592	first is that pullback commutes with the tensor product for \emph{all}
2593	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the canonical comparison
2594	\(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N\) is an isomorphism for every \(M, N\)
2595	and every \(f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}, the general strong-monoidality of
2596	pullback). The second is that pullback preserves the structure sheaf,
2597	\(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, already in
2598	hand). Given an inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), pulling back
2599	yields
2600	\[
2601	  f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N
2602	    \;\cong\; f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2603	    \;\cong\; f^*\mathcal{O}_X
2604	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2605	\]
2606	so \(f^*N\) is a tensor inverse of \(f^*M\). The general comparison
2607	\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} is therefore a genuine isomorphism and the
2608	load-bearing input to \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback}.
2609	
2610	\paragraph{Why the general isomorphism is genuine content, not free.} The comparison is
2611	\emph{not} a formal consequence of pullback being \emph{some} (op)lax monoidal functor.
2612	An (op)lax monoidal functor need not preserve invertible objects: global sections
2613	\(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal, yet \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not an
2614	invertible module, so the bare oplax comparison \emph{map} carries no invertibility
2615	information. One must genuinely prove that the comparison \(\delta\) is an
2616	\emph{isomorphism}. This is precisely why \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} is real work
2617	and not a corollary of the oplax comparison map
2618	\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}: the oplax structure supplies \(\delta\) as a
2619	morphism for free (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}), but its invertibility is the entire
2620	content.
2621	
2622	\paragraph{The construction route.} The isomorphism is built on a
2623	\emph{concrete} model of the pullback and transported to the project's abstract
2624	\(f^* = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\) along the uniqueness of left
2625	adjoints --- the same \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) device that closed
2626	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. The construction decomposes into four steps, carried
2627	out in the proof of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} and the sub-lemmas it cites:
2628	\begin{itemize}
2629	  \item[(D1)] \emph{Decomposition.} The presheaf pushforward factors as a fixed-ring
2630	    pushforward followed by restriction of scalars, so its left adjoint --- the
2631	    presheaf pullback --- factors as a topological inverse image \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\)
2632	    followed by extension of scalars (\cref{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition}).
2633	  \item[(D2)] \emph{Scalar half is strong (free).} Extension of scalars is strong
2634	    monoidal via the base-change distributivity isomorphism
2635	    \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange}\); so the comparison
2636	    \(\delta\) for the pullback is an isomorphism \emph{if and only if} the comparison
2637	    \(\delta_0\) for \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is.
2638	  \item[(D3)] \emph{Topological half --- the genuine build.} \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is
2639	    the left Kan extension along \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\);
2640	    pointwise its value at an open \(V\) is a colimit over the comma category
2641	    \((F \downarrow V) = \{U : f^{-1}V \subseteq U\}\). This category is up-directed
2642	    (\(U_1 \cup U_2\) is an upper bound), and the tensor product commutes with filtered
2643	    colimits, so \(\delta_0\) is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback0_tensor_iso}).
2644	  \item[(D4)] \emph{Sheafify and transport.} Sheafify the resulting presheaf-level
2645	    isomorphism --- the unit and tensor factors are reconciled with their sheafified
2646	    forms by the sheafification-monoidality brick \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) ---

2648	    \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\) along \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\),
2649	    yielding \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}.

2810	      \;\cong\;
2811	    (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S),
2812	  \]
2813	  an isomorphism for \emph{every} ring map \(R \to S\), with no flatness. Composing a
2814	  strong-monoidal functor with \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\), the comparison \(\delta\) for
2815	  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is therefore an isomorphism \emph{if and only if} the
2816	  comparison \(\delta_0\) for \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is. This isolates the topological
2817	  half as the sole genuine obligation.
2818	
2819	  \emph{Step 3 (D3: the topological half --- the genuine build).} The functor
2820	  \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is the left Kan extension
2821	  \(\mathrm{Lan}\,(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\) on underlying
2822	  presheaves (its right adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\) is restriction along
2823	  \(F^{\mathrm{op}}\)). Pointwise, with \(F = (\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\),
2824	  \[
2825	    \mathtt{pullback}_0(M \otimes N)(V)
2826	      = \operatorname*{colim}_{(F \downarrow V)} \bigl(M(U) \otimes N(U)\bigr),
2827	    \quad
2828	    (\mathtt{pullback}_0 M \otimes \mathtt{pullback}_0 N)(V)
2829	      = \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} M(U)\Bigr) \otimes \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} N(U)\Bigr).
2830	  \]
2831	  The comma category \((F \downarrow V) = \{U : f^{-1}V \subseteq U\}\) is up-directed
2832	  --- \(U_1 \cup U_2\) is an upper bound of any \(U_1, U_2\) --- and the tensor product
2833	  commutes with filtered colimits (the diagonal \((F\downarrow V) \to (F\downarrow V)
2834	  \times (F \downarrow V)\) is final). Hence the comparison \(\delta_0\) is an
2835	  isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback0_tensor_iso}). This is the genuine content of the lemma.
2836	
2837	  \emph{Step 4 (D4: sheafify and transport).} Steps 1--3 furnish a strong-monoidal
2838	  structure on the concrete presheaf-level inverse image. Sheafify the resulting presheaf
2839	  isomorphism to the sheaf level --- the unit and tensor factors are reconciled with
2840	  their sheafified forms by the sheafification-monoidality brick
2841	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\), the same \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
2842	  shape used in \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. By definition
2843	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\)
2844	  is \emph{a} left adjoint of the pushforward; the uniqueness of left adjoints
2845	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) supplies a canonical natural isomorphism from it
2846	  to the concrete model, along which the strong-monoidal structure travels. Its
2847	  tensorator becomes the asserted isomorphism
2848	  \[
2849	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2850	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2851	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
2852	  \]
2853	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\), refining the comparison map
2854	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
2855	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}; the abstract pullback is never evaluated sectionwise.
2856	
2857	  No shortcut from the bare oplax map is available: the presheaf pullback is oplax
2858	  monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) and so supplies \(\delta\) as a
2859	  morphism for free, but an (op)lax monoidal functor does not preserve invertible objects
2860	  --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal yet \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1,
2861	  \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible --- so the isomorphism must be proved, which is
2862	  exactly the work of Steps~1--4.
2863	\end{proof}

2879	  \[
2880	    \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi
2881	      \;\cong\;
2882	    \mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{pullback}_0,
2883	    \qquad
2884	    \mathtt{pullback}_0 := (\mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R).\mathtt{leftAdjoint},
2885	  \]

2887	  extension along \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\) --- and
2888	  \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi\) is sectionwise extension of scalars along the

2908	\begin{lemma}
2909	[The topological inverse image commutes with $\otimes$ (filtered-colimit interchange)]
2910	  \label{lem:pullback0_tensor_iso}
2911	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback0TensorIso}
2912	  \uses{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition}
2913	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) be the
2914	  topological inverse image of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules of
2915	  \cref{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition} --- the left Kan extension along
2916	  \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\). For arbitrary presheaves of
2917	  modules \(M, N\), the canonical comparison
2918	  \[
2919	    \delta_0 :
2920	    \mathtt{pullback}_0(M \otimes N)
2921	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2922	    \mathtt{pullback}_0 M \otimes \mathtt{pullback}_0 N
2923	  \]
2924	  is an isomorphism, natural in \(M, N\).
2925	\end{lemma}
2926	
2927	\begin{proof}
2928	  \uses{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition}
2929	  Evaluate both sides pointwise. As a left Kan extension, \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) has the
2930	  pointwise colimit formula
2931	  \[
2932	    \mathtt{pullback}_0(P)(V)
2933	      \;=\;
2934	    \operatorname*{colim}_{(F \downarrow V)} P(U),
2935	    \qquad F = (\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}},
2936	  \]
2937	  over the comma category \((F \downarrow V)\), whose objects are the opens \(U\) of
2938	  \(X\) with \(f^{-1}V \subseteq U\). Since the presheaf tensor product is sectionwise,
2939	  \((M \otimes N)(U) = M(U) \otimes N(U)\), one has
2940	  \[
2941	    \mathtt{pullback}_0(M \otimes N)(V)
2942	      = \operatorname*{colim}_{(F \downarrow V)} \bigl(M(U) \otimes N(U)\bigr),
2943	    \quad
2944	    \bigl(\mathtt{pullback}_0 M \otimes \mathtt{pullback}_0 N\bigr)(V)
2945	      = \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} M(U)\Bigr) \otimes \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} N(U)\Bigr),
2946	  \]
2947	  and \(\delta_0(V)\) is the canonical comparison between them. The comma category
2948	  \((F \downarrow V)\) is up-directed: it is nonempty (the whole space \(X\) contains
2949	  \(f^{-1}V\)) and any two members \(U_1, U_2\) have the upper bound \(U_1 \cup U_2\),
2950	  still containing \(f^{-1}V\). Over a filtered (here up-directed) index the tensor
2951	  product commutes with the colimit --- the diagonal \((F\downarrow V) \to (F\downarrow V)
2952	  \times (F\downarrow V)\) is final, so the colimit of the pointwise tensors agrees with
2953	  the tensor of the colimits --- whence \(\delta_0(V)\) is an isomorphism for every \(V\),
2954	  and \(\delta_0\) is an isomorphism of presheaves, natural in \(M, N\).

2956	  The pointwise left-Kan-extension model of \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) and the
2957	  filtered-colimit/tensor interchange for \(\mathcal{O}\)-module-valued presheaves are not
2958	  in Mathlib; this lemma is therefore established bottom-up from the
2959	  left-Kan-extension colimit formula and the filtered-colimit preservation of the
2960	  tensor product.
2961	\end{proof}

\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is then a three-line consequence. The comparison
is needed \emph{only} on locally-trivial pairs --- the carrier never presents a
general \(\mathcal{O}\)-module --- so no comparison for arbitrary modules is

\paragraph{The reduction to two general facts.} The target
\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is a three-line consequence of two facts about
pullback, exactly as in the Stacks proof of \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}. The
first is that pullback commutes with the tensor product for \emph{all}
\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the canonical comparison
\(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N\) is an isomorphism for every \(M, N\)
and every \(f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}, the general strong-monoidality of
pullback). The second is that pullback preserves the structure sheaf,
\(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, already in
hand). Given an inverse \(N\) with \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), pulling back
yields
\[
  f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N
    \;\cong\; f^*(M \otimes_X N)
    \;\cong\; f^*\mathcal{O}_X
    \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
\]
so \(f^*N\) is a tensor inverse of \(f^*M\). The general comparison
\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} is therefore a genuine isomorphism and the
load-bearing input to \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback}.

\paragraph{Why the general isomorphism is genuine content, not free.} The comparison is
\emph{not} a formal consequence of pullback being \emph{some} (op)lax monoidal functor.
An (op)lax monoidal functor need not preserve invertible objects: global sections
\(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal, yet \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not an
invertible module, so the bare oplax comparison \emph{map} carries no invertibility
information. One must genuinely prove that the comparison \(\delta\) is an
\emph{isomorphism}. This is precisely why \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} is real work
and not a corollary of the oplax comparison map
\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}: the oplax structure supplies \(\delta\) as a
morphism for free (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}), but its invertibility is the entire
content.

\paragraph{The construction route.} The isomorphism is built on a
\emph{concrete} model of the pullback and transported to the project's abstract
\(f^* = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\) along the uniqueness of left
adjoints --- the same \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) device that closed
\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. The construction decomposes into four steps, carried
out in the proof of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso} and the sub-lemmas it cites:
\begin{itemize}
  \item[(D1)] \emph{Decomposition.} The presheaf pushforward factors as a fixed-ring
    pushforward followed by restriction of scalars, so its left adjoint --- the
    presheaf pullback --- factors as a topological inverse image \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\)
    followed by extension of scalars (\cref{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition}).
  \item[(D2)] \emph{Scalar half is strong (free).} Extension of scalars is strong
    monoidal via the base-change distributivity isomorphism
    \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange}\); so the comparison
    \(\delta\) for the pullback is an isomorphism \emph{if and only if} the comparison
    \(\delta_0\) for \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is.
  \item[(D3)] \emph{Topological half --- the genuine build.} \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is
    the left Kan extension along \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\);
    pointwise its value at an open \(V\) is a colimit over the comma category
    \((F \downarrow V) = \{U : f^{-1}V \subseteq U\}\). This category is up-directed
    (\(U_1 \cup U_2\) is an upper bound), and the tensor product commutes with filtered
    colimits, so \(\delta_0\) is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback0_tensor_iso}).
  \item[(D4)] \emph{Sheafify and transport.} Sheafify the resulting presheaf-level
    isomorphism --- the unit and tensor factors are reconciled with their sheafified
    forms by the sheafification-monoidality brick \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) ---
    then transport the strong-monoidal structure from the concrete model to
    \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\) along \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\),
    yielding \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}.
\end{itemize}
The substantial work is concentrated in (D3): the filtered-colimit/tensor interchange for
\(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\)-valued presheaves and the concrete pointwise model of
\(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) are not in Mathlib and require a bottom-up construction. The
remaining inputs --- \(\mathtt{distribBaseChange}\), \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\),
\(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\), and the comparison \emph{map}
\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} --- are standard.
input.new_string:
\paragraph{The reduction to two facts about pullback.} The corollary
\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is a three-line consequence of two facts about
pullback, exactly as in the Stacks proof of \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}. The
first is that pullback commutes with the tensor product on the \emph{locally-trivial}
pairs the consumer presents: the canonical comparison
\(f^*(L \otimes_X L') \cong f^*L \otimes_Y f^*L'\) is an isomorphism whenever
\(L, L'\) are locally trivial, for every \(f\)
(\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}). The second is that pullback preserves the
structure sheaf, \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\)
(\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, already in hand). Given a locally-trivial inverse
\(N\) with \(L \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), pulling back yields
\[
  f^*L \otimes_Y f^*N
    \;\cong\; f^*(L \otimes_X N)
    \;\cong\; f^*\mathcal{O}_X
    \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
\]
so \(f^*N\) is a tensor inverse of \(f^*L\). The locally-trivial comparison
\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} is the load-bearing input.

\paragraph{Why the apparent obstruction does not apply.} It is tempting to argue that
the comparison can never be an isomorphism because an (op)lax monoidal functor need
not preserve invertible objects --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal, yet
\(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible. But that
counterexample concerns the \emph{lax} tensorator of \emph{pushforward}: \(\Gamma\)
is a right adjoint (global sections), and its comparison
\(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{G}) \to \Gamma(\mathcal{F} \otimes
\mathcal{G})\) genuinely fails to be an isomorphism. The comparison we need is the
\emph{oplax} \(\delta\) of \emph{pullback}, a \emph{left} adjoint, running the other
way, \(f^*(M \otimes N) \to f^*M \otimes f^*N\). Pullback \emph{provably preserves
local triviality} (\cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}): if \(M\) is trivialised
by \(\mathcal{O}_U\) on a cover \(\{U_i\}\), then \(f^*M\) is trivialised by
\(\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}U_i}\) on \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) --- using only
\(f^*\mathcal{O} \cong \mathcal{O}\) and the compatibility of pullback with
restriction along open immersions. Consequently, on locally-trivial objects, both
sides of \(\delta\) are locally trivial, and \(\delta\) restricts to a comparison
between the structure sheaf and itself on each chart, where it is forced to be an
isomorphism. The bare oplax structure supplies \(\delta\) as a \emph{morphism} for
free (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}); the entire remaining content is the
chart-local check that, on a trivialising cover, \(\delta\) is an isomorphism.

\paragraph{The construction route.} The isomorphism is obtained by \emph{upgrading
the already-built oplax comparison map} \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} =
\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) to an isomorphism
through a chart-chase --- the same shape as the proven local-triviality results
\cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback} and
\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, and \emph{not} via any concrete
inverse-image model or filtered-colimit construction. The argument decomposes into
four steps, carried out in the sub-lemmas cited by
\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}:
\begin{itemize}
  \item[(D1$'$)] \emph{Naturality of \(\delta\) in \((M,N)\).} The sheaf-level
    comparison \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is natural in both arguments, assembled from
    the naturality of the abstract oplax \(\delta\) (Mathlib's
    \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\delta\mathtt{\_natural}\)) and of the
    sheafification reconciliations used to build it
    (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}). This naturality is what lets a chart
    replace \(M|_{U_i}, N|_{U_i}\) by the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}\) without
    changing whether \(\delta\) is an isomorphism there.
  \item[(D2$'$)] \emph{\(\delta\) on the unit pair \((\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})\) is an
    isomorphism.} On the trivial bundle the comparison
    \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})} : f^*(\mathcal{O} \otimes \mathcal{O}) \to
    f^*\mathcal{O} \otimes f^*\mathcal{O}\) is identified, through the unitors and the
    structure-sheaf comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\)
    (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, an isomorphism for \emph{all} \(f\)) and the Mathlib
    monoidal-unit coherences
    \(\delta\mathtt{\_comp\_}\eta\mathtt{\_tensorHom}\) /
    \(\delta\mathtt{\_comp\_tensorHom\_}\eta\), with an isomorphism; hence
    \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})}\) is an isomorphism
    (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}).
  \item[(D3$'$)] \emph{\(\delta\) commutes with the open-immersion base-change square}
    (the genuinely new ingredient). For an open \(U_i\) of \(X\) with preimage
    \(f^{-1}U_i\), let \(g_i : f^{-1}U_i \to U_i\) be the restriction of \(f\) and
    \(j_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\), \(j_i' : f^{-1}U_i \hookrightarrow Y\) the open
    immersions, so \(f \circ j_i' = j_i \circ g_i\). The comparison \(\delta\)
    intertwines the restriction-to-\(f^{-1}U_i\) of \(\delta\) for \(f\) with the
    \(\delta\) for \(g_i\), through the pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism. This
    is the tensorator analog of the already-built unit coherence
    \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}, established by the same mate calculus
    (Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp\_}\delta\) together with the
    conjugation \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\) of the pseudofunctor
    isomorphisms) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}).
  \item[(D4$'$)] \emph{Chart-chase assembly.} Choose a common affine cover \(\{U_i\}\)
    of \(X\) trivialising both \(M\) and \(N\) (the common refinement, exactly as in
    \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). On each \(f^{-1}U_i\), the
    base-change coherence (D3$'$) aligns the restriction of \(\delta\) with the
    \(\delta\) for \(g_i\); naturality (D1$'$) replaces the trivialised
    \(M|_{U_i}, N|_{U_i}\) by \(\mathcal{O}\); and (D2$'$) makes the resulting
    comparison on the unit pair an isomorphism. Thus \(\delta\) restricts to an
    isomorphism on every member of the cover \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) of \(Y\), and the
    restriction-detects-isomorphism criterion
    \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict} promotes it to a global isomorphism. The
    structure mirrors the chase of \cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}.
\end{itemize}
The only genuinely new sub-step is (D3$'$); (D1$'$) is Mathlib naturality, (D2$'$)
is the unit coherence backed by \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\), and (D4$'$) reuses the
already-proven cover machinery
(\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial},
\cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}). No concrete inverse-image model, no
filtered-colimit/\(\otimes\) interchange, and no general comparison for arbitrary
modules is constructed.

\begin{lemma}
[Naturality of the sheaf-level pullback--tensor comparison $\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}$ (D1$'$)]
  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap_natural}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. The sheaf-level comparison map
  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is \emph{natural} in both module arguments: for
  morphisms \(a : M \to M'\) and \(b : N \to N'\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
  the square
  \[
    \begin{array}{ccc}
      f^*(M \otimes_X N)
        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}
        & f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N \\[2pt]
      \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*(a \otimes b)}
        & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*a \,\otimes\, f^*b} \\[4pt]
      f^*(M' \otimes_X N')
        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}
        & f^*M' \otimes_Y f^*N'
    \end{array}
  \]
  commutes.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  The map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is built (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) by
  transporting the abstract oplax comparison \(\delta\) of the presheaf pullback
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) through the sheafification
  reconciliations. Each ingredient is natural: the abstract oplax \(\delta\) is natural
  in both arguments --- this is Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\delta\mathtt{\_natural}\), valid for any oplax
  monoidal functor --- and the sheafification, the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
  comparison, and the unit/tensor reconciliation \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) are
  natural transformations. The asserted square is the pasting of these naturality squares,
  so it commutes; naturality in the two arguments is checked separately and then combined,
  the tensor of the two component morphisms factoring as \(f^*a \otimes f^*b\) by
  bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_Y\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[The comparison on the unit pair $(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})$ is an isomorphism (D2$'$)]
  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. On the monoidal unit pair
  \((\mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the sheaf-level comparison
  \[
    \delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})} :
      f^*(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X)
      \;\longrightarrow\;
      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_Y f^*\mathcal{O}_X
  \]
  of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is an isomorphism.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  The structure-sheaf comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f : f^*\mathcal{O}_X
  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y\) is an isomorphism for \emph{every} \(f\)
  (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}); abbreviate it \(\eta\). Tensoring its source and
  target with the monoidal unitors of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,Y\), the right-hand
  side \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_Y f^*\mathcal{O}_X\) is identified, via
  \(\eta \otimes \eta\) and the right (equivalently left) unitor
  \(\mathcal{O}_Y \otimes_Y \mathcal{O}_Y \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\), with
  \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X\); and the left-hand side
  \(f^*(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X)\) is identified, via the image under
  \(f^*\) of the unitor \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\),
  with \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X\). Under these two identifications \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},
  \mathcal{O})}\) becomes the identity of \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X\): this is precisely the
  monoidal-unit coherence of an oplax monoidal functor relating \(\delta\) to the unit
  comparison \(\eta\) via the unitors --- Mathlib's
  \(\delta\mathtt{\_comp\_}\eta\mathtt{\_tensorHom}\) and
  \(\delta\mathtt{\_comp\_tensorHom\_}\eta\). Since \(\eta\) and the unitors are
  isomorphisms and \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})}\) is conjugate to the identity by
  them, \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})}\) is an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

% NOTE: D3' is the SOLE genuinely-new sub-step of the loc-triv route. It is the
% tensorator analog of the already-built, axiom-clean unit coherence
% lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp, established by the same mate calculus
% (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp_delta + conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv). Feasibility is
% demonstrated by that unit analog.
\begin{lemma}
[The comparison commutes with the open-immersion base-change square (D3$'$)]
  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorMap_restrict}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
        lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes, let \(U \subseteq X\) be an open with
  preimage \(f^{-1}U \subseteq Y\), and write \(g : f^{-1}U \to U\) for the restriction
  of \(f\), with open immersions \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) and
  \(j' : f^{-1}U \hookrightarrow Y\), so that \(f \circ j' = j \circ g\). Then the
  sheaf-level comparison \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}
  for \(f\) and the comparison for \(g\) agree across the base-change square: for all
  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) the restriction
  \((j')^*\bigl(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\bigr)\) coincides, through the pullback
  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) of the equality
  \(f \circ j' = j \circ g\), with \(\delta^{g}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j^*M, j^*N)\). In
  particular the restriction of \(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) to \(f^{-1}U\) is an
  isomorphism if and only if \(\delta^{g}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j^*M, j^*N)\) is.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp,
        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
  This is the tensorator analog of the unit-comparison composition coherence
  \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}, and is proved by the same mate calculus rather
  than on sections (the left-adjoint pullback exposes no sectionwise value). The oplax
  comparison \(\delta\) of a composite of left adjoints decomposes through the
  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism: Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp\_}\delta\) expresses the \(\delta\) of a composite
  oplax monoidal functor as the whiskered composite of the \(\delta\)'s of the factors.
  Applying this to the two factorisations of the underlying functor of the square
  \(f \circ j' = j \circ g\) --- as \((j')^* \circ f^*\) and as \(g^* \circ j^*\) ---
  and conjugating the pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphisms by the
  adjunction mate correspondence (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) for the left adjoints
  corresponds to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) for the right adjoints, via
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\)) identifies the two restricted
  comparisons. The restriction along the open immersion \(j'\) is the strong-monoidal
  restriction functor of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, so it carries
  \(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) to its value on the restricted modules without
  introducing a defect; the displayed identification of
  \((j')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) with \(\delta^{g}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) follows.
  The final clause is then immediate, an isomorphism being preserved and reflected by the
  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism it is conjugated by.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ on locally-trivial pairs (D4$'$)]
  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial}
  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{locally trivial}
  (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}). Then the sheaf-level comparison map
  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is an isomorphism:
  \[
    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
  \]

  it is established by promoting the already-built map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) to an

  filtered-colimit machinery.

  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{lemma}
  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$

  % \label{lemma-pullback-locally-free}

  % be a morphism of ringed spaces. If $\mathcal{G}$ is

  % $f^*\mathcal{G}$ is a locally free $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
  % \end{lemma}"
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemmas
  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}:
  pullback commutes with \(\otimes\) functorially, and the pullback of a locally free
  (locally trivial) module is again locally free; on locally-trivial pairs the comparison
  is therefore an isomorphism, checkable on a trivialising cover.}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Lemmas \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and
  \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}.}

  affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both restrict to the trivial bundle,
  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(N|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) --- the
  common refinement of the two trivialising covers, exactly as in the proof that the
  tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). The preimages
  \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) form an open cover of \(Y\). It suffices, by the
  restriction-detects-isomorphism criterion \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, to show
  that the canonical map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on
  each \(f^{-1}U_i\); the canonical global map being shown locally-iso is exactly what
  lets us conclude (a non-canonical patchwork of local isomorphisms would not glue).

  Fix \(i\) and write \(g_i : f^{-1}U_i \to U_i\) for the restriction of \(f\), with open
  immersions \(j_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) and \(j_i' : f^{-1}U_i \hookrightarrow Y\),
  so \(f \circ j_i' = j_i \circ g_i\). The base-change coherence
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange} identifies the restriction
  \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) with the comparison
  \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) for \(g_i\) on the restricted modules,
  and reduces the claim to: \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an
  isomorphism. On \(U_i\) the restricted modules are trivial, \(j_i^*M \cong
  \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(j_i^*N \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); by naturality of
  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) in both arguments (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural})
  these isomorphisms transport \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) to the
  comparison on the unit pair \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(\mathcal{O}_{U_i},
  \mathcal{O}_{U_i})\). That comparison is an isomorphism by
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit} --- the unit-pair case, where the structure-sheaf
  comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\) (an isomorphism for all \(g_i\)) and the unitors
  do the work. Hence \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an isomorphism,
  so is its base-change identification \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\), and
  thus \(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on each
  \(f^{-1}U_i\). (Both sides are indeed locally trivial on this cover --- \(f^*M, f^*N,
  f^*(M\otimes_X N)\) are locally trivial by \cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback} and
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- which is what makes the chart-local
  comparison a map between copies of the structure sheaf.) By
  \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) is a global
  isomorphism, natural in \(M, N\) by \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}.
\end{proof}
